title: digitalis × macedonica
intro: Digitalis × macedonica, also known as Macedonian foxglove, is a hybrid plant derived from the Digitalis purpurea and Digitalis lutea species, producing tall spikes of tubular, foxglove-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a cardiac glycoside, increasing the force of heart contractions and improving cardiac function. The health benefits of Digitalis × macedonica include its potential to treat heart failure, arrhythmias, and other cardiovascular conditions. Digitalis × macedonica can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried extract, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, but its use requires careful monitoring and prescription by a qualified healthcare professional due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
